description.en: Evergreen tree widely grown in Sri Lankan Low-country home gardens; boiled breadfruit is a staple main meal traditionally consumed with scraped coconut or coconut sambol, and curry preparations with coconut milk are common. Cultivated varieties are largely seedless; seeded breadfruit varieties are grown mainly for their edible seeds. Vegetatively propagated trees begin fruiting in 3–6 years. Yields vary from 5.5 t/ha (traditional agroforestry) up to 16–50 t/ha under orchard conditions; 150–250 fruit or 160–500 kg per tree per year typical.

duration_days.quote: Vegetatively propagated trees start fruiting in 3–6 years

expected_yield_kg_per_acre.quote: Average fruit yield is 5.5 t/ha under traditional agroforestry system on the island of Pohnpei (Micronesia), and ranges as high as 16 to 50 t/ha under orchard conditions. Studies in Barbados indicate a reasonable potential of 15 to 30 t/ha
expected_yield_kg_per_acre.review_notes: 5.5–30 t/ha → 2225–12141 kg/acre spread across management systems.

description.quote: In Sri Lanka, breadfruit is cooked as a curry using coconut milk and spices (which becomes a side dish) or boiled, with boiled breadfruit being a famous main meal. It is often consumed with scraped coconut or coconut sambol
